LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS SUTTER HEALTH AND SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS' DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT - THE DEMURRER TO THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENT INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS IS OVERRULED. SEE OCHOA V. SUPER. CT. (1985) 39 CAL.3D 159. THE DEMURRER TO THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF THE UCL IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF CAN OBTAIN RESTITUTION AND POSSIBLY AN INJUNCTION IF THE MISCONDUCT IS CONTINUING. SEE IN RE TOBACCO II CASES, 46 CAL.4TH 298, 321. IN MAKING THIS RULING, THE COURT HAS CONSIDERED ONLY THE ALLEGATIONS IN THE PLEADINGS AND NOT ANY FACTS SET FORTH SOLELY IN PLAINTIFF'S MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 = (302/MJM)

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS SUTTER HEALTH AND SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS' MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT - THE MOTION TO STRIKE IS DENIED. AS THE COURT PREVIOUSLY RULED, THE SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT STATES A CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF THE CLRA AND THAT CLAIM SUPPORTS A CLAIM FOR PUNITIVE DAMAGES. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 = (302/MJM)

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-26-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JOSHUA H. WATSON OF THE DOLAN LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 421-2800 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS SUTTER HEALTH AND SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS' DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT - The demurrer to the 3rd cause of action for negligent infliction of emotional distress is overruled. See Ochoa v. Super. Ct. (1985) 39 Cal.3d 159. The demurrer to the 5th cause of action for violation of the UCL is overruled. Plaintiff can obtain restitution and possibly an injunction if the misconduct is continuing. See In re Tobacco II Cases, 46 Cal.4th 298, 321. In making this ruling, the Court has considered only the allegations in the pleadings and not any facts set forth solely in plaintiff's memorandum of points and authorities. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 = (302/MJM)

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-26-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. JOSHUA H. WATSON OF THE DOLAN LAW FIRM, TEL. NO. 421-2800 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS SUTTER HEALTH AND SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS' MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT - The motion to strike is denied. As the Court previously ruled, the Second Amended Complaint states a cause of action for violation of the CLRA and that claim supports a claim for punitive damages. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 = (302/MJM)

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-06-2013 Continued To Feb-26-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter.
RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS SUTTER HEALTH, SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS' MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS Continued on the court's own motion to February 26, 2013. Judge: Marla Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-06-2013 Continued To Feb-26-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter.
RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS SUTTER HEALTH, SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS' DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS Continued on the court's own motion to February 26, 2013. Judge: Marla Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Anthony Vaughn #6185.

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-10-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Joshua H. Watson, Esq. and Justin Lim, Esq., 415.421.2800, for plaintiff Adriana Uribe.
Susan O. Fish, Esq., 408.288.9700, for Defendants Sutter Health and Sutter Health West Bay Hospitals DBA California Pacific Medical Center/St Luke's Campus.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling: DEFENDANTS SUTTER HEALTH and SUTTER WEST BAY HOSPITALS DBA CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER/St Luke's Campus'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled as to the second cause of action and sustained, with 10 days leave to amend, as to the third and fifth causes of action. Order signed in open Court. Judge: A. James Robertson II, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077.
